title: Eschweilera subglandulosa
taxonomy: Eschweilera subglandulosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Lecythidaceae. This plant is classified within the genus Eschweilera, which is part of the family Lecythidaceae. The family Lecythidaceae is a part of the order Ericales, which is a larger clade of flowering plants. The order Ericales is also part of the clade asterids, which is one of the two main clades of flowering plants. The plant's classification is part of a larger system of plant taxonomy that groups plants into hierarchical categories based on shared characteristics.

intro: Eschweilera subglandulosa, also known as the "Brazilian cherry" or "cupuaçu", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America. The health benefits of Eschweilera subglandulosa include its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce inflammation, as well as its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Eschweilera subglandulosa is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
